must_have_breakdown.skill: Salesforce Platform Management or Salesforce Architecture
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Candidate managed Salesforce org security, environment strategy, and release lifecycle but is titled 'DevOps Engineer' without explicit platform architecture or enterprise-wide platform ownership scope.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Expert-level proficiency with Copado for CI/CD and release orchestration
must_have_breakdown.evidence: At TCS, candidate 'owned end-to-end Salesforce release lifecycle using Copado across Dev, QA, UAT, and Production environments' and holds Copado Fundamentals I, II, and Copado Certified Consultant certifications.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Deep understanding of Salesforce multi-tenant architecture
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume mentions metadata API, org strategy, and profiles/permission sets but never explicitly references multi-tenant architecture concepts or governor limits knowledge.
Candidate 'administered Salesforce org security including profiles, permission sets, login IP policies, and environment management' and managed Dev, QA, UAT, and Production environments.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Communication skills to translate technical releases into business-friendly roadmaps
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Candidate 'coordinated with product owners, QA, and engineering teams' but no evidence of translating technical releases into business-friendly roadmaps or executive-level communication.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Experience managing Business Critical production environments
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Candidate managed production deployments and 'improved deployment success rate' but no explicit mention of Business Critical tier or Salesforce premier support engagement.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Working with Salesforce premier support tiers

must_have_breakdown.evidence: Candidate has SFDX, Salesforce CLI, and metadata API experience but the background is DevOps-oriented rather than hands-on Salesforce development of custom objects, triggers, or Apex.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Custom objects, workflows, triggers, SOQL & SOSL

must_have_breakdown.evidence: Candidate enforced 'deployment governance, quality checks, and security scans' and applied structured validation but no explicit mention of Force.com design patterns or application architecture.
Resume shows ownership of end-to-end release lifecycle but no explicit evidence of independent decision-making without supervision in a senior capacity.

reasoning: Neeraj is a competent Salesforce DevOps Engineer with strong Copado expertise and solid CI/CD pipeline experience, but the role requires a Senior Salesforce Platform Manager with broader platform architecture ownership, Apex/SOQL development depth, governor limits knowledge, and business roadmap communication skills. His profile is heavily DevOps and release-engineering focused rather than platform strategy or development. Three must-haves are outright missing (governor limits, premier support, custom objects/SOQL/SOSL), and five others are only partially evidenced. He lacks the architectural scope, declarative/programmatic development background, and business-stakeholder communication skills that define this senior manager role.
strengths: Copado expertise is deep and certified: Candidate holds Copado Fundamentals I, II, and Copado Certified Consultant, and owned end-to-end release lifecycle using Copado at TCS for over 4 years — directly matching the JD's primary tooling requirement.

Proven CI/CD pipeline design: Candidate designed scalable GitHub Actions and Jenkins pipelines for Salesforce SFDX projects and achieved a 30-40% reduction in deployment time, demonstrating measurable pipeline engineering value.

Strong DevOps governance practices: Candidate enforced deployment governance, quality gates via Checkmarx and PMD, rollback strategies, and structured validation — aligning with the JD's need for release integrity and production stability.

Multi-tool Salesforce DevOps coverage: Proficiency across Copado, Gearset, Flosum, DevOps Center, and SFDX demonstrates broad ecosystem familiarity, reducing onboarding time for Salesforce-adjacent tooling.

Salesforce Administrator certification and security management: Candidate holds the Salesforce Certified Administrator credential and managed profiles, permission sets, and login IP policies, providing foundational platform administration competence.
gaps: No evidence of Salesforce development skills (Apex, SOQL, SOSL, triggers, custom objects): The JD explicitly requires working in the Salesforce development environment with these technologies, and the resume contains zero mentions of any programmatic development work.

No platform architecture or Center of Excellence experience: The role requires architecting a federated development strategy and establishing a CoE; the candidate's profile is execution-level DevOps, not strategic platform architecture.

Missing governor limits and multi-tenant architecture knowledge: These are listed must-haves that are entirely absent from the resume, suggesting the candidate has not operated at the platform architecture layer needed for this role.

No experience with Salesforce premier support tiers or Business Critical environments: The JD explicitly calls out working with Salesforce premier support, which requires a different seniority of engagement; this is absent from the resume.

No evidence of translating technical work into business roadmaps: A core JD responsibility is communicating complex releases to business stakeholders; the candidate only mentions coordination with product owners, which falls short of senior stakeholder management.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period is specified by the JD or stated by the candidate, so no penalty applies.
JD work_structure is not specified as In-office, so no location penalty is applied.
Candidate has 5 years of experience, which meets the JD's minimum relevant_experience of 5 years.
JD gender_preference is 'Any', so no penalty applies.
No college preference or exclusion is specified in the JD, so no adjustment applies.
